site.tagline: Inspiring Growth. Empowering Success. Leading with Empathy.
site.subtitle: Inspiring Growth. Empowering Success. Leading with Empathy.
site.footerNote: Built with Heart, Purpose, and Passion

introStatement: I am a collaborative and people-centered leader who values inclusivity, trust, and growth. I am passionate about creating environments where individuals feel heard, supported, and empowered to reach their full potential. Through my background in organizational leadership, caregiving, and mentorship, I bring empathy, clear communication, and a strong commitment to helping others succeed. I believe effective leadership is not just about guiding teams, but about uplifting others and creating meaningful, lasting impact.

education.degree: Bachelor of Applied Science, BAS-OLTM - Organizational Leadership & Technical Management
education.description: Focused on leadership, strategy, collaboration, and inclusive organizational growth.

education.degree: Associate of Applied Science in Medical Administration
education.description: Built strong administrative, communication, and healthcare systems knowledge.

workExperience.description: Serve as a peer mentor to 200+ college students, helping Scholars to navigate academic and professional resources, supporting them in college and WSOS processes, sharing opportunities, and building a sense of community.
Support quadriplegic patients with daily living activities, physical care, emotional well-being, and greater independence in the home environment.
Oversaw day-to-day operations, led a team of approximately 15 employees, managed hiring and training, and improved workflow, service quality, and inventory control.

skills: Ability to Multitask and Manage Multiple Projects

projects.title: Capstone
projects.description: My Capstone project represents the culmination of my journey through the Organizational Leadership and Technical Management (OLTM) program, integrating my digital portfolio, leadership challenge, and service project. It highlights my growth from an introverted team member who values empathy, collaboration, and adaptability. My digital portfolio includes this website, a leadership mission blog, and links to professional platforms. My leadership challenge is based on The Leadership Challenge by Kouzes and Posner (2023), applied through weekly discussions and reflections on my role as a Career and Technical Scholar Lead at WSOS, where I mentor over 200 college students. My service project involves volunteering with WSOS for 3 hours per week over 8 weeks, supported by detailed logs and journals that connect my experiences to course concepts. These elements reflect my personal growth, turning challenges like impatience and cross-cultural communication into strengths that shape my leadership style.

projects.title: COIL Project: Final Group Presentation on COIL & Social Media Influences
projects.description: The Collaborative Online International Learning (COIL) Project was a five-week virtual collaboration between students from Lower Columbia College (LCC) and the University of Sharjah in the United Arab Emirates. This project focused on exploring cultural differences in digital identity and social media use, allowing us to compare perspectives shaped by distinct cultural and societal norms. Through cross-cultural collaboration, I developed stronger communication skills, particularly in navigating time zones, language nuances, and differing viewpoints. I also strengthened my leadership and teamwork skills by contributing to group discussions and coordinating our final presentation.

projects.title: Progressive Journal
projects.description: This Journal is a window into my growth as a leader throughout the Developing and Leading High Performance Teams course. It is an important artifact because it reflects my growth as a collaborative leader. In this journal, I examine my strengths, including being organized, empathetic, and goal-oriented, while also identifying areas where I can continue to improve, such as patience and allowing others more opportunities to lead.

projects.title: Leadership Portfolio
projects.description: My Leadership Portfolio, from the Transformative Leadership and Decision-Making course, is one of the most meaningful artifacts because it presents my personal philosophy of leadership. In this portfolio, I explain that leadership is not about authority or control, but about empowering others, building trust, creating an inclusive environment, and helping people reach their full potential.

projects.title: Workforce Communication Assessment
projects.description: My Workforce Communication Assessment, from the Professional Organizational Communications course, is an important artifact because it demonstrates my ability to evaluate communication practices within an organization and identify opportunities for improvement. In this assessment, I analyzed the communication structure, culture, stakeholder relationships, and challenges within the Cowlitz Youth Soccer Association.

projects.title: Shark Tank
projects.description: My Shark Tank project, from the Small Group Communication course, is relevant to my capstone because it demonstrates the importance of communication, structure, and teamwork in achieving a common goal. In this project, my group quickly established roles, set communication norms, and worked together to complete the assignment effectively. I served as the coordinator, enabling me to apply my organizational skills to keep the team focused and on track.

references.name: Aaliyah Hänni
references.notes: Aaliyah Hänni collaborated with me as the web developer for a soccer camp website, where I served as the project liaison and coordinated all aspects of the camp and site development. She can speak to my organizational skills, coordination abilities, and leadership in managing cross-functional projects.

references.name: Jessica Vilai
references.notes: Jessica Vilai oversees my work as a Career & Technical Scholar Lead with Washington State Opportunity Scholarship and can speak to my leadership, communication, and program involvement.

references.notes: I worked closely with Cathy Reynolds while volunteering as Treasurer for Cowlitz Youth Soccer Association, where she mentored me and supported my development in managing financial tasks and organizational responsibilities.

references.name: Mark Gaither
references.connection: Instructor, academic advisor, and Rotaract Club advisor at Lower Columbia College
